title: The One With The Hypnosis Tape

lines.text: Central Perk, all but Chandler are there, Joey laughs for no apparent reason.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Joey) What's so funny?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, nothing, no. It's an acting exercise, I'm practising my fake laugh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh. (she laughs)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What-what's so funny?
Chandler enters with a cigarette.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: (to Chandler) Oh, no-no, no-no-no, there's none of that in here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh come on man! At least let me finish this last one.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Okay, but only if you give me a drag.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.
Chandler hands him the cigarette, and he takes a long drag.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Oh dark mother, once again I suckle at your smokey tit. (hands Chandler back the cigarette.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no, why don't you hang on to that one.
He goes and sits down next to Rachel and puts a cigarette in his mouth, which Rachel takes away from him. He puts another cigarette in his mouth, and Rachel takes it away again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, that's like the least fun game ever.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I'm really sick of your smoking, so I brought something that is going to help you quit. (hands him an audio cassette)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh. (takes the cassette and puts it on his arm like the stop smoking patch, and it falls off.) Nope, that patch is no good. (Joey and Monica both do their fake laughs.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on, it's a hypnosis tape. This woman at work used it for two weeks straight and she hasn't smoked since.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pffhah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Ross) What's your problem?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nothing, it's just that hypnosis is beyond crap.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, I watched you get hypnotised in Atlantic City.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, that guy did not hypnotise me! Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh right, 'cause you always pull your pants down at the count of three and play Wipe-out on your butt cheeks.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, y'know forget hypnosis. The way to quit smoking is you have to dance naked in a field of heather, and then bath in the sweat of six healthy young men.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Or what my Father called Thursday night.
Opening Credits
Central Perk, continued from earlier, Ross is handing Rachel a cup of coffee.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, y'know what, I didn't want cinnamon on this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sorry. (To remedy that, Ross scoops the cinnamon off of the top with his hand.)

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: (entering) Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God!!

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Frank! Hi!

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: How are you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Oh, well y'know, I would've called but I lost your phone number and then ah, my Mom locked me out of the house so I couldn't find it. And then, I tried to find a pay phone, and ah, the receiver was cut off. So...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Ah, oh, the ah, vandalism.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But, also, what happened between you and your Mom?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Well, we got into a fight 'cause ah, she said I was to immature to get married.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Your getting married?!

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Oh, yeah!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: My little brother's getting married!!

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Oh, I knew you'd be so cool about this. All right, ah, hey, do you want to meet her?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Do I?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Do you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I do, yeah.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Okay, cool, all right, she just ah, parking the truck. (to Joey) I'm gonna, I'm gonna get my ah, my fianc? man!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, I would've bet good money that he'd be the first one of us to get married.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, isn't it fantastic?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, ah, but Pheebs don't you think he's a little young to get married?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What, he's 18.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Exactly, it'll be illegal for him to drink at his own bachelor party.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, or-or to get a hooker.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Always illegal Joe.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: (entering with his fianc? Alice, who is obviously much, much older than he is) Hey! Hey! This is ah, my fianc?, Mrs. Knight. (he points out Phoebe to her)

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Y'know it-it's funny, um, Frank told me so much about you, but your not how I pictured you at all.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I'm a big surprise.
Ross lets them both sit in his chair.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, um, how-how did you guys meet?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Well um, I was in ah Mrs. Knight's ah, I mean Alice, sorry, Alice, I always do that. I was in her ah, Home Ec class.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: And he was my best student.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Yeah, she was my best teacher.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Ohhh. (They embrace in a very passionate kiss.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If that doesn't keep kids in school, what will?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And so now you guys are gonna be married?

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Yeah. Y'know we-we talked about just living together, but um, we want to have kids right away.
Both Chandler and Phoebe have shocked looks on their faces.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God!! Great!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow, kids. Frank, are you sure you're ready for that?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: I mean, how hard can it be? Y'know, I mean, y'know, babies, y'know who doesn't want babies right? And besides y'know, I never had a Dad around, and ah, now-now I always will, 'cause y'know, it'll be me. Right?

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Y'know, I mean, really we do realise that there's an age difference between us.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh good! Okay. 'Cause you were acting like you didn't.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Oh no, but when it comes to love, what does age matter?
They both growl and hiss at each other and then kiss passionately again.
Chandler's bedroom, Chandler is listening to the hypnosis tape.

lines.speaker: Hypnosis Tape
lines.text: You are falling fast asleep. Deeper. Deeper. Deeper. You are now completely asleep. You don't need to smoke. Cigarettes don't control you. You are a strong, confident woman, who does not need to smoke. A strong, confident woman.
The Moondance Diner, Monica is working, Rachel is having lunch.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And y'know what, I just realised, in the last year I've only gone out with two guys, Richard and Julio. You gotta help me out here, you gotta set me up, you gotta get me back in the game.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, that shouldn't be a problem. I mean I work in fashion and all I meet are eligible straight men.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to a customer) Pete, can I get you something else?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Yeah, a slice of cheesecake and-and a date if you're given' 'em out.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Haven't you and I covered that topic?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Hmm, come on, you just said to her that you....

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Aww, the only reason you want to go out with me because my blond wig, and the big boobs, and the fact that I serve you food.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Well, if that were true, I'd dating my Aunt Ruth. And the two times we went out were just plain awkward. (to Rachel) Come on, you think she should go out with me, don't you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (laughs) Well, I mean, are you sure you want to go out with her? I mean that ain't a pretty picture in the morning, y'know what I mean. That wig all in disarray, and boobs flung over the night stand, y'know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I mean really, think about it.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Ho-ho, I will.
Chandler and Joey's, Ross and Joey are being lectured by Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I know, I know, that this is Frank's life, (walks behind them, they turn around in the leather chairs to face her) y'know. Y'know, I don't want to be all judgmental, y'know, but this is sick, it's sick and wrong!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pheebs, what, is it the age thing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-no, oh, I'm fine with the age thing y'know, until it starts sticking it's tongue down my little brother's throat!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pheebs, he seems to enjoy it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But, I mean, do you think he's gonna enjoy it when he's up to his elbows in the diapers from all the babies they have to have right away?! This is not fair to Frank, (she walks behind them again, and hey again turn to follow her) and it-it's not fair to the babies, and y'know what, it's not good home economics.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, have-have you told him how you feel?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes. Not out loud.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pheebs, if you don't tell him, soon he's gonna be married, and then you're gonna hate yourself.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, but if I do tell him, then he's gonna hate myself. I mean look at him and his Mom, I can't. (pause) But, you guys can, please you gotta talk him out of it.

lines.speaker: Ross and Joey
lines.text: No-no-no-no-no. (They start to turn away, but Phoebe stops them, and turns them back to face her.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Come on, you guys, you have nothing to lose, I have everything to lose. Do you want me to lose everything? Everything?!

lines.speaker: Ross and Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I'm gonna go get Frank. (exits)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So, we're walking down the street and I turn to you and I say, "Hey, let's go hang out at Totally Nude Nudes," remember? And then, and then, you turn to me and say, "Nah, let's just hang out at your place." Well, that was a nice move dumb ass.
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel and Monica are entering.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I think you should definitely go out with this guy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nah, he doesn't do anything for me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica, last Saturday night, what happened on Walker: Texas Ranger?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, umm, Walker was looking for this big bus load of kids.... (realises) All right, I get your point.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering, carrying a briefcase) Hi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, I forgot the combination to this about a year ago? I just carry it around. Do you have any Chap Stick?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh, yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, how are those tapes working out for ya?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what, pretty good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Good! I haven't smoked yet today, I feel great, and-and-and confident, that is a stunning blouse.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thanks

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey Mon, let's give Pete a chance Come on, he was funny, he seems really nice, and that check thing was adorable.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What check thing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: As a joke, this customer at work who has a crush on me gave me a $20,000 tip. His number is on the check, he just did so I'd call him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (reading the check) Pete Becker. Pete... (quickly grabs a magazine and opens it up to show her a picture) (pointing to the picture) Is this him?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's Bill Clinton.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Who's he huggin'?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! That's Pete! But why is Bill huggin' Pete?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This guy invented Moss 865! Every office in the world uses that program!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We use it!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There you go!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God, Monica's gonna go out with a millionaire.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm not gonna go out with him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God, I can't believe this is a real $20,000 check, oh this is just so exciting.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Or incredibly offensive.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah, sure, that too.
Chandler is putting on the Chap Stick the same way that women put on lipstick, including the bit with the piece of tissue.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to the girls who are staring at him) What?
Chandler and Joey's, Ross and Joey are talking to Frank.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All we're saying is don't rush into anything.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, come on, think about it. You're 18, okay, she's 44, when you're 36, she's gonna be 88.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: What, you don't think I know that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, the point is, there's a lot of women out there you haven't even had sex with yet!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, he-he's right, he's right. This is your time y'know, yeah, you're young, you're-you're weird, chicks dig that.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Okay, but isn't sex better when it's with one person that you really, really care about.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, in a poem maybe.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No the man's right, that's what I had with Rachel.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: You don't have it anymore?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I ah, I slept with someone else.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Okay, so wait, all right, so how does that make things better?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It didn't.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Okay, so what you used to have with Rachel, is what I've got with Alice.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Now, wh-what, what is that like?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: It's so cool man, it's so, it's just 'cause being with her is so much better than like not being with her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Ross) Why can't I find that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Don't ask me, I had it and I blew it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I want it!

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: You can have it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know, maybe I can't. I mean, maybe there's something wrong with me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, no! No!

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: It's out there man! I've seen it! I got it!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Then you hold on to it!!

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: All right, man!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, congratulations you lucky bastard! (hugs him)
Chandler and Joey's, it's after Ross and Joey's talk with Frank, and Phoebe's is finding out what happened.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Joey) You're Frank's best man?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I couldn't help it, there love is so pure.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well then, (to Ross) what about you?! Huh?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm the ring bearer.

As Phoebe stands there in shock and disbelief, Chandler comes out of the bathroom and walks to his bedroom. He's just got out of the shower and has the towel wrapped around himself high across his chest, and another towel wrapped around his head, like women wear towels. Joey watches Chandler wondering what the hell he's doing.
Commercial Break
Phoebe's, Phoebe is opening the door.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi! Oh, Alice, hi! Thanks. I'm so glad you could come, 'cause I've got a real umm, Home Ec emergency. (Points to the table cloth, which has a huge mustard stain on it.)

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Oh my God, who died on this?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I know. It's a real mustard-tastrophe. Can you help me?

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Absolutely. Okay, first we'll start with a little club soda and salt, and then if that doesn't work we can go back to...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know what, forget it. It's ruined.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Oh no-no, never say that. If we can't get it out then we can cut around the stain, add a little lace, you make a stylish throw.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Or instead, maybe you could just not marry my brother Frank.
Pete's office, he is participating in a conference call with three other employees, each of whom are on a different TV screen that he controls with a remote.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Okay, that's great, but can we make it smaller? Can we make it fit on the head of a pin? I love when we make things fit on the head of a pin.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Got it. Yeah all right. Yeah, okay.
The intercom buzzes.

lines.speaker: Secretary
lines.text: You have a Miss Monica Geller here.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Uh, absolutely, yeah, sEnd her in. (Monica enters) Hi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What the hell is this? (holding up the check)

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Hang on a second. (to the employees) I'll-I'll talk to you in the morning. (turns two of the three off) I'm sorry what?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Seriously, what is this supposed to mean?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Well, y'know, I never know how much to tip.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're supposed to double the tax. Not double the tax of Romania. I mean, what's-what's the deal? Are you, are you trying to buy me? Is this the way you get girls to go out with you?

lines.speaker: Employee
lines.text: Umm, I'm still here.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: (turns off the TV) You're taking this all wrong. Because, if I didn't leave you that tip, you wouldn't of come down here, we wouldn't be having this argument, and there wouldn't be this ah, heat between us.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Come on, you gotta admit that our relationship is ah, is hitting a new level now. 'Cause you used to be like the chef, and I was the customer, and now we're like this-this couple that fights.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, umm, you're a loon.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Look, forget the check, okay. (rips up the check) I like you. I think you're great. Come on, what do you say?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: 'Cause I don't want to encourage this kind of behaviour.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: One meal! That's all I'm asking for. Please? We go out, we eat, and if you don't have a good time, I give you ten grand, we call it even.
Phoebe's, she is coming home. She turns on the lights, and sits down on the couch.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: (hiding under a pile of clothes) Hi. (She jumps up screaming.) Wait, no! Just put the mail down. It's-it's me!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Whoa, sorry. Why were you just like all in the dark?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Oh well, um, your, your laundry just smelled so good, that I thought I'd curl up in it. Is that all right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yeah, sure. So, how was your day?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Oh, well just probably the worst one since I've been alive.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What umm, what happened?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Umm, Alice ah, she ah, called it off.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no. Did umm, did she say why?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Uh, no, not really, just that I was too young, y'know, but I don't see how I could all of the sudden be too young, 'cause I'm older than I was when we first got together.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, no, I don't, I don't know. But, y'know what, maybe it's just all for the best?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Yeah, if the best is like unbelievable pain!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, sweetie, oh. (hugs him)

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Y'know, I just was finally happy y'know. For the first time in my life! After my Dad left me, and then, and then getting arrested for stealing those birds, and then, and then the whole punctured lung thing! I can, it's still really hard to take deep breaths in cold weather, but with Alice all that stuff kinda went away. And now it's, and now it's gone and I don't know why!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh, well I can tell you why. It's, it's because of me. But, y'know what, I only did it because I love you. Okay?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, well I, I kinda had a little chat with Alice, and I sort of made her see why you two shouldn't be together, y'know. And you're gonna see it to, one day, you really, really will.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Wait a minute, wait, this is because of you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Well, you, wait no, my Mother didn't want us to be together, but the worst thing she ever did was tie me to the porch.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, but.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Wait, y'know what, I-I came to you because I thought you'd understand! Oh no!! Y'know, I would storm out of here right now if-if I had some money, or a place to go...
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is getting Monica ready for her date. The guys are also there. The door buzzes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! The millionaire's here!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (in a feminine way) Oh my God! (and he jumps all around)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Guys, please, I'm just gonna have dinner with him. Okay?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, okay, just because he buys you dinner, does not mean you owe him anything.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, then get the lobster!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (opening the door) (to Pete) Hey!

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi!
They're all staring at him, with big, huge smiles on their faces.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, how much cash do you got in your pocket right now?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And that's why, I'm not inviting you in for a drink. (starts to leave) Bye.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh-no-no-no-no....

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Just one drink?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (in the hallway) So, where do you want to go?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Hey, you like pizza?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, that's sounds great.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: I know a great little place.
[Cut to a shot of the coliseum in Rome, Italy.]
A restaurant in Rome, Monica is paying for the pizza.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: You're, hey, you're not paying for the pizza!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh come on, it's only fair, you paid for the flight. Now is, is that enough lire?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Ahh, I'd throw another thousand on that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why, how much is that?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: That's about 60 cents.
Chandler and Joey's, it's the middle of the night. Joey is walking into the living room, and runs into the entertainment centre.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Every night!!
He starts to walk to the bathroom and hears the hypnosis tape from Chandler's bedroom.

lines.speaker: Hypnosis Tape
lines.text: You do not need to smoke. Cigarettes don't control you. You are a strong, confident woman, who does not need to smoke. A strong, confident woman, who does not need to smoke. (Joey walks out smiling to himself.)
Phoebe's, Frank is watching TV, and he's very depressed as Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, Frank. Look, okay, I know that you think I did like this totally evil thing, but I so didn't. There's someone here who can explain this better than I can.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Hi Frank.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Hi, Mrs. Knight.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Phoebe's right Frank. I know it's hard to hear, but it would've been wrong to go through with it. I-I-I was being selfish, even though we, we want the same things now, in the future we may not. (to Phoebe) Is that it, is that what it is?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, but not just that.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Right, not just that. Umm, even though we love each other as much as we do, none the less...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: None the less.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: None the less. Umm, you're too young to, to really know what you want. (They embrace in a passionate kiss.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's right, exactly. (sees them) All right, it's a good bye kiss, that's good. (Frank picks Alice up and they move to the couch) Bye-bye. (They both lie down on the couch and start to make out.) Okay, no, the important thing is that you see what I'm saying, y'know, just y'know, this is clearly wrong. (They ignore her) Okay, I've decided I'm gonna let this happen! Okay, can I just get my purse? (She reaches in and Alice moans) Okay, all right, good. (leaves).
Closing Credits
Chandler's bedroom, he's listening to the hypnosis tape again.

lines.speaker: Hypnosis Tape
lines.text: Cigarette's don't control you. You are a strong, confident woman, who does not need to smoke.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (He's recorded his voice on the tape) Joey's your best friEnd. You want to make him a cheese sandwich everyday. (he laughs) And you also want to buy him hundreds of dollars worth of pants.
Chandler wakes up and stares at the tape.
End
